allies.rationale_text: Critical-mass helps compounding distinguish between early accumulation and the threshold where gains start accelerating.
Feedback-loops explain how repeated gains or losses become self-reinforcing over time.
allies.source_quote: continuous improvement is achieved through feedback loops

allies.rationale_text: Margin-of-safety gives compounding the downside protection needed to preserve the base that future gains depend on.
allies.source_quote: protecting downside is as important as amplifying upside

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias turns compounding dangerous when people keep scaling a flawed thesis instead of checking whether the foundation is wrong.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Compounding conflicts with optionality when commitment to a compounding path forecloses pivots that would redirect effort toward a higher-yielding trajectory.
structured_tensions.source_quote: compounding conflicts with optionality (optionality) when commitment to a compounding path forecloses pivots that would redirect effort toward a higher-yielding trajectory.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Compounding conflicts with margin-of-safety when projecting compound growth rates forward without accounting for the fragility of the base that sustains them.
structured_tensions.source_quote: compounding conflicts with margin-of-safety (margin of safety) when projecting compound growth rates forward without accounting for the fragility of the base that sustains them.
